source: Generated from a per-system Morphous manifest and prompt records. CSS variables are shadcn/tweakcn-compatible.
typography: Compact English incident-response UI with tabular metrics, severity labels, timeline entries, and Noto Sans JP guidance for Japanese command-center, alert, handoff, and containment screens.
layout: Dense 8px incident grids, basalt navigation rails, severity cards, event timelines, service tables, containment checklists, command palettes, lava-crack separators, pumice texture samples, and restrained 8px cards.

prompts.label: Transparent lava tube tunnel motif
prompts.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous Lava Tube transparent motif chroma-key source
Primary request: Create only a lava tube tunnel motif for a design-system catalog, isolated as a single complete volcanic tunnel subject on a removable chroma-key background.
Subject: A compact cutaway-like basalt lava tube tunnel segment from a volcanic island: arched black basalt tube mouth, rugged cooled lava walls, grey pahoehoe ridges, fractured basalt ledges, subtle orange molten glow from within the tunnel floor and rear cracks, mineral specks, soot-dark texture, and a clear tunnel silhouette. The identity must read as lava tube tunnel, not cave generic, mine shaft, sewer, subway tunnel, volcano cone, or fantasy dungeon.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; include the full arched tube opening, inner tunnel depth, black basalt edges, grey cooled lava textures, and orange glow without cropping. Clean studio lighting that preserves rock detail. No cast shadow.
Transparent workflow: place the subject on a perfectly flat solid #00ff00 chroma-key background for removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, lighting variation, or texture. Do not use #00ff00 anywhere in the subject.
Constraints: no people, no equipment, no signs, no plants, no ocean, no sky, no landscape background, no text, no watermark, no extra cave entrance, no fantasy crystals, no lava waterfall.

prompts.label: Light-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Lava Tube light-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive light-mode design-system board for a Morphous theme named "Morphous Lava Tube", derived from a lava tube tunnel motif. This board is a source reference for implementing a shadcn/tweakcn-compatible incident response system.
Canvas: exact 16:9 design-system board, 3840 x 2160 composition, high-resolution 4K-class source board, clean professional UI design-system presentation. Use large readable labels and avoid dense tiny text.
Required visible sections: Morphous Lava Tube, Lava Tube Tunnel, Palette, Typography, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Incident Dashboard / Data, Forms, Table, Command / Search, Status States, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadow, Tokens, Asset Examples.
Reference use: Extract colors and interaction language from an arched black basalt lava tube, rugged cooled lava walls, grey pahoehoe ridges, fractured basalt ledges, orange molten glow in cracks, soot-dark texture, volcanic island geology, tunnel depth, and emergency operations context. The board must read as lava tube tunnel, not generic cave, mine, sewer, volcano cone, fantasy dungeon, or black-and-orange gaming UI.
Typography: English incident response UI examples with Japanese typography guidance; labels such as Geist, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, Noto Sans JP, Japanese UI, headings, body, labels, numbers. Use compact bilingual incident, alert, asset, timeline, handoff, and command-center labels with tabular numbers.
UI coverage: primary/secondary/ghost/destructive buttons, hover/pressed/disabled/loading states, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, incident severity cards, on-call roster, event timeline, containment checklist, service table rows, command search palette, sidebar navigation, dashboard metric cards, line chart, bar chart, donut chart, empty/loading/error states, progress, stepper, date picker, filters, status chips, spacing scale, radius samples, border, shadow, texture samples, simple icons, theme token swatches.
Style: incident response system, precise 8px grid and 8px card radius, ash-white and pale grey operational surfaces, black-basalt navigation rails and dividers, molten-orange primary actions and critical status, cooled-grey secondary controls, smoke-grey muted panels, pumice texture overlays, lava-crack focus rings, dense but scannable dashboards, polished shadcn/tweakcn component language.
Color palette: black basalt, orange glow, cooled grey, ash white, smoke grey, pumice silver, ember red only for destructive or critical states. Keep the palette restrained and motif-derived.
Constraints: no generic purple or blue gradients, no neon arcade colors, no beige-only palette, no fantasy crystals, no cave tourism poster, no random brand names, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Lava Tube", no tiny unreadable paragraphs.

prompts.label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Lava Tube dark-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive dark-mode design-system board for the Morphous theme named "Morphous Lava Tube" / "Lava Tube Tunnel". Translate the same light design-system language into dark mode; do not invent a new unrelated theme.
Canvas: exact 16:9 design-system board, 3840 x 2160 composition, high-resolution 4K-class source board, clean professional shadcn/tweakcn presentation. Keep labels large and legible.
Required visible sections: Morphous Lava Tube, Lava Tube Tunnel, Dark Mode, Palette, Typography, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Incident Dashboard / Data, Forms, Table, Command / Search, Status States, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadow, Tokens, Asset Examples.
Reference use: Preserve black basalt navigation rails, rugged cooled lava surfaces, grey pahoehoe texture, molten-orange primary actions, ember-red critical states, ash-white foreground text, smoke-grey muted panels, lava-crack focus rings, volcanic tunnel depth, incident severity cards, on-call roster, event timeline, containment checklist, service table, command search, status chips, and an 8px shadcn/tweakcn grid. The board must read as lava tube tunnel, not generic cave, mine shaft, sewer, volcano cone, fantasy dungeon, or black-and-orange gaming UI.
Typography: English incident response UI examples with Japanese typography guidance; labels such as Geist, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, Noto Sans JP, Japanese UI, headings, body, labels, numbers. Use compact bilingual incident, alert, asset, timeline, handoff, and command-center labels with tabular numbers.
UI coverage: primary/secondary/tertiary/ghost/destructive buttons, hover/pressed/disabled/loading states, checkbox, radio, switch, input, select, textarea, tabs, badges, alerts, incident severity cards, service health cards, on-call roster, event timeline, containment checklist, service table rows, line chart, bar chart, donut chart, sidebar navigation, command search palette, filters, empty/loading/error states, progress, stepper, date picker, texture samples, radius samples, spacing scale, shadow samples, token code snippets, icon set.
Visual details: basalt-black app background, deep tunnel panels, cooled-grey surfaces, ash-white labels, molten-orange active states and focus glow, ember-red critical alerts, pumice-grey borders, subtle lava-wall texture overlays, thin glowing crack separators used sparingly, restrained contrast, crisp incident-response dashboards.
Color palette: black basalt, basalt deep, cooled grey, smoke grey, pumice silver, ash white, molten orange, ember orange, magma red. Keep the palette restrained and motif-derived.
Constraints: no neon arcade UI, no generic pure-black flat dashboard, no unrelated blue/purple gradient dominance, no fantasy crystals, no cave tourism poster, no random brand names, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Lava Tube", no tiny unreadable paragraphs.
